issue.title: Deno panics when run in pm2
issue.body:
Version: Deno 2.2.11
Windows Server 2019
PM2 version 5.4.2

When running 

`pm2 start myapp.ts —interpreter=”deno” —interpreter-args=”run —allow-all”`

in PowerShell as admin I get this


```
Deno has panicked. This is a bug in Deno. Please report this
at https://github.com/denoland/deno/issues/new
If you can reliably reproduce this panic, include the
reproduction steps and re-run with the RUST_BACKTRACE=1 env
var set and include the backtrace in your report.

Platform: windows x86_64
Version: 2.2.11
Args: [“deno”, “run”, “--allow-all”, “C:\\deno\\myapp.ts”]
View stack trace at:
https://panic.deno.com/v2.2.11/x86_64-pc-windows-msvc/o5y6Tg_imoCyhgwoC-2_voC8-ywoCiprlhEkn4xTst_wTmri1Sg764Tw8-3DqupoV639zHqmn7T-0pzDsmqoV4ozrgEos9Fis0W
thread ‘main’ panicked at runtime\worker.rs.743:7:
Bootstrap exception: Error: The handle is invalid. (os error 6)
 at Object.setupChildProcessIpcChannel [as__setupChildProcessIpcChannel] (node:child_process:492:14)
 at initialize (ext:deno_node/02_init.js:45:15)
 at bootstrapMainRuntime (ext:runtime_main/js/99_main.js:968:7)
note: run with `RUST_BACKTRACE=1` environment variable to display a backtrace
```

NOTE:
The above text was run through OCR, but I have   tried being thorough when looking through the result.
I have changed the name of the app in the above text.
